blocks.content:
The condition is the boundary between normal and firing. Your query returns data continuously; the condition decides which of those values mean something is wrong.

Without a well-chosen condition, an alert either stays quiet when it shouldn't or floods you with notifications during normal operation. The right operator and threshold give you time to respond before a situation becomes critical.

A good threshold reflects what "normal" looks like for your system. If your CPU usually runs at 40-60%, alerting at 80% gives you warning headroom.

In the next milestone, you learn how to choose an operator and set your threshold.
